Christ our righteous King, and his true disciples, are clearly intended here. The consolations and graces of his Spirit are like rivers of water in this dry land; and just as an overhanging rock provides refreshing shade and shelter to a weary traveler in the desert, so his power, truth, and love give the believer the only real protection and refreshment during the difficult journey to heaven. Christ bore the storm himself to keep it off from us. Let the trembling sinner flee for refuge to him; for he alone can protect and refresh us in every trial. See how much effort sinners put into sin; they work at it, their hearts are focused on it, and with skill they plan wickedness; but this is our comfort: they cannot do more harm than God allows. Let us seek to have our hearts freed from selfishness. A generous person plans generous things concerning God, and desires that He will grant wisdom and prudence, the comforts of his presence, the influence of his Spirit, and in due time the enjoyment of his glory.

When so much provocation was given to holy God, bad times might be expected. Sadly, how many careless people there are who support self-indulgence with shameful stinginess! We deserve to be deprived of the supports of life when we use them to feed our desires. Let such tremble and be troubled. Blessed times will come through the pouring out of the Spirit from on high; then, and not until then, there will be good times. The present state of the Jews will continue until a more abundant outpouring of the Spirit from on high. Peace and quietness will be found in the way and work of righteousness. True satisfaction is to be had only in true religion. And real holiness is real happiness now, and will be perfect happiness, that is, perfect holiness forever. The good seed of the word will be sown in all places, and watered by Divine grace; and laborious, patient laborers will be sent forth into God’s husbandry.
